content.problemStatement:
Given an array of numbers, find the minimum number of operations needed to make it palindromic, where in each operation you can select any two adjacent elements, add them, and replace them with their sum.

Note: A palindromic array reads the same forwards and backwards.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of testcases.
Each test case contains two lines,

The first line contains the number of elements in the array NNN.
Second line contains NNN space separated integers




Output Format

Output the minimum number of operations needed to make the array palindromic

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: [1, 2, 3, 4]

Operation 1: Add 1,2 -> [3, 3, 4]
Operation 2: Add 3,4 -> [3, 7]
Final array [3, 7] is not yet palindromic
Operation 3: Add 3,7 -> [10]
Final array [10] is palindromic (single element is always palindromic)
Total operations = 3
